mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressure, which compresses corporate profit margins and reduces equity valuations. A 0.3 percentage point or greater monthly decline in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% as of June 2017 near cyclical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Negative UNRATE changes do not produce significantly lower 20-day forward returns than positive UNRATE changes (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings. A quarter-over-quarter increase in GDPC1 of 2.5% or greater annualized rate predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations expand with earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% in preceding quarter
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters do not produce significantly higher 60-day forward returns than negative growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05), or correlation between GDPC1 growth and subsequent SPX returns is not statistically significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 1.5% combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.3%) signals monetary tightening cycle. This dual condition triggers equity repricing as real discount rates rise and growth expectations decline, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.04% and CPIAUCSL at 243.79 as of June 2017
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Triggered instances do not produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth rate decelerates below 1.5% annualized over a 3-month rolling window, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints. This deceleration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical equity sectors reprice downward.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration below 1.5% does not produce significantly lower 5-day forward returns than periods of faster growth (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, signaling synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand. This joint weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline across consumer-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 473513M and HOUST at 1092K units in May 2017
regime_hint: demand-side recession or sharp demand contraction
failure_condition: Simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST declines do not produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100,000 jobs per month over a 3-month rolling average, signaling labor market weakening. This deceleration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risk increases and equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146404K persons in June 2017 with strong recent growth
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deceleration approaching recession
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth below 100K threshold does not produce significantly lower 60-day forward returns than periods above threshold (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate rises above 2.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% annualized, creating positive real rates that compress equity valuations. This regime of restrictive real monetary policy predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as discount rates applied to future cash flows increase.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates becoming restrictive as Fed normalizes policy
failure_condition: Triggered instances do not produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the real rate signal (FEDFUNDS minus inflation) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 1,200,000 annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 3% year-over-year, signaling synchronized strength in residential and consumer demand. This dual expansion predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as cyclical sectors reprice upward on accelerating growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1200K annualized AND RSAFS YoY growth > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1092K and RSAFS at 473513M in May 2017
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand strength
failure_condition: Triggered instances do not produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression versus single-series models over any 2-year rolling window.
